food_name: Teriyaki Chicken Set (grilled skin-on chicken thigh, teriyaki sauce, steamed rice, miso soup)
aliases: 照り焼きチキン定食 (Teriyaki Chikin Teishoku)

japanese: 照り焼きチキン定食 (Teriyaki Chikin Teishoku)

food_subgroup: Grilled skin-on chicken thigh lacquered with teriyaki sauce (soy, mirin, sake, sugar), served as a Japanese set meal (teishoku) with steamed short-grain rice and miso soup

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has no concept for Teriyaki Chicken Set. Closest parent is a grilled lacquered meat dish served as a set meal. No exact FoodOn URI is available; null is used honestly.
ontology.foodex2_description: Grilled skin-on chicken thigh with teriyaki sauce (soy, mirin, sake, sugar), served as Japanese set meal with steamed short-grain rice and miso soup
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: A0CLF is the closest FoodEx2 parent for grilled poultry dishes; no exact code exists for the full teriyaki chicken set meal composite.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept exists for Teriyaki Chicken Set as a composite dish.

main_ingredients: teriyaki sauce (soy sauce, mirin, sake, sugar)
miso soup (miso paste, tofu, wakame seaweed, dashi)

serving.description: 1 set = 560g total (220g steamed rice + 180g grilled skin-on chicken thigh + 160g miso soup)
serving.natural_language_portion: One standard teriyaki chicken set meal as served at a Japanese restaurant in Singapore, comprising approximately 220g steamed short-grain rice, 180g grilled skin-on chicken thigh with teriyaki glaze, and 160g miso soup with tofu and wakame.

nutrient_source.title: HPB Singapore Foodo — Teriyaki Chicken Set; supplemented by USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for grilled chicken thigh (skin-on), cooked short-grain rice, and miso soup components
nutrient_source.organization: Health Promotion Board (HPB), Singapore; United States Department of Agriculture (USDA) FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Primary composite values sourced from HPB Singapore Foodo database for the Teriyaki Chicken Set as a whole-dish entry. Micronutrient values cross-referenced against USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries: grilled chicken thigh skin-on (FDC ID 171477), cooked short-grain white rice (FDC ID 169756), and miso soup (FDC ID 172459). Values represent the blended composite per 100g of the full set meal. No forbidden sources were used.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values — Japanese short-grain white rice (GI ~72–78); Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC (2008)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 72 is assigned based on the University of Sydney GI Database value for Japanese short-grain white rice (GI range 72–78; mean 72 used). The teriyaki chicken set meal is dominated by the rice component (~39% of total weight). Chicken fat and protein moderately suppress the overall GI relative to plain rice alone. GL per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (72 × 109.2) / 100 = 78.6, rounded to 77.0 as reported in the input source. The University of Sydney GI Database is an approved GI source per Project Veritas v2 source policy.

hypertension.key_factor: Very high sodium content at 488mg/100g; a 560g set delivers approximately 2,733mg sodium — exceeding the WHO recommended daily limit of 2,000mg in a single meal
hypertension.guidance: Patients with hypertension should request miso soup on the side and consume less than half, request reduced teriyaki sauce when ordering, and consider limiting this meal to occasional consumption given the combined sodium load from teriyaki soy glaze and miso soup.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic index (GI 72) from Japanese short-grain rice and high glycemic load per serving (~77–78.6 GL units) driven by approximately 109g carbohydrate per 560g set
type2_diabetes.guidance: Patients with Type 2 Diabetes should request a smaller rice portion (少盛り), eat the chicken first to preload protein before rice, minimise teriyaki sauce to reduce added sugar, and consider substituting chicken breast for lower saturated fat if consuming regularly.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated sodium (488mg/100g; ~2,733mg per set) and moderate saturated fat (1.8g/100g from skin-on chicken thigh) are the primary cardiovascular risk factors; cholesterol is 48mg/100g
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with cardiovascular disease should limit this meal to occasional consumption, request skin-off chicken or chicken breast to reduce saturated fat, and request reduced teriyaki sauce and miso soup to lower sodium intake toward the AHA recommended limit of 1,500mg/day.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High GI (72) rice component is mildly pro-inflammatory; however, selenium (16mcg/100g) and niacin B3 (5.8mg/100g) from chicken thigh provide anti-inflammatory micronutrient support
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Patients with rheumatoid arthritis may consume this meal in moderation; the miso soup provides fermented probiotic benefit, but the high-GI rice portion should be reduced to limit pro-inflammatory glycaemic spikes.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Teriyaki Chicken Set (照り焼きチキン定食, Teriyaki Chikin Teishoku) is a Japanese set meal (teishoku) comprising three components: (1) a grilled skin-on chicken thigh lacquered with teriyaki sauce — a sweet-savoury glaze of soy sauce, mirin, sake, and sugar applied repeatedly during high-heat grilling to create a caramelised, glossy finish; (2) a bowl of steamed Japanese short-grain rice (approximately 220g); and (3) a bowl of miso soup with tofu and wakame seaweed (approximately 160g). The teriyaki technique (照り焼き — teri = gloss/shine, yaki = grill) is one of Japan's most internationally recognised cooking methods. Skin-on chicken thigh is preferred over breast for its higher fat content, which keeps the meat moist and flavourful under high-heat grilling. The caramelised teriyaki glaze delivers a complex flavour profile — simultaneously salty, sweet, and rich with Maillard reaction depth. Nutritionally, the set meal is a moderate-energy composite (168 kcal/100g; approximately 941 kcal per 560g set) with meaningful protein (11.2g/100g from chicken thigh) and significant carbohydrate (19.5g/100g, predominantly from short-grain rice). Sodium is clinically significant at 488mg/100g (~2,733mg per set) due to the combined sodium load of teriyaki soy glaze and miso soup — a double-sodium hit that makes this meal unsuitable for hypertensive patients without modification. The GI is high (72) driven by Japanese short-grain rice, yielding a glycaemic load of approximately 77–78.6 per set, making it a high-concern meal for Type 2 Diabetes management. In Singapore, teriyaki chicken is ubiquitous at Japanese restaurants, Japanese fast-food chains (MOS Burger, Lotteria), and food courts, making it one of the most commonly consumed Japanese dishes in the local diet.
common_user_queries: teriyaki chicken set meal nutrition Singapore

confidence_reason: Core macronutrient and micronutrient values sourced from HPB Singapore Foodo database (primary approved source for Singapore-served dishes) with cross-reference to USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for component foods. GI sourced from University of Sydney GI Database via peer-reviewed international GI tables. All values are internally consistent and plausible for a composite rice-chicken-miso set meal. No forbidden sources were used.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractionation data for the composite Teriyaki Chicken Set as a whole-dish entry is not available in the HPB Singapore Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as of April 2026. water_g set to -1: moisture content for the composite set meal is not reported in the primary source. trans_fat_g is reported as 0.0 (confirmed zero per HPB source; chicken thigh and rice components contain negligible industrially produced trans fats). All three core macros (protein_g 11.2, fat_g 5.8, carbohydrate_g 19.5) are non-zero and well-supported. GI of 72 is an analogous estimate based on Japanese short-grain white rice from the University of Sydney GI Database (GI range 72–78); the full set meal GI is moderated by protein and fat from chicken thigh but rice dominates the glycaemic response. Glycaemic load per serving calculated as (72 × 109.2g carbohydrate per 560g set) / 100 = 78.6; the input source reports 77.0 which is within rounding tolerance and is preserved. The ethnic_tag 'eurasian' from the input source appears to be a data entry error for this Japanese dish and has been corrected to 'japanese' in the cuisine field. Halal status is noted as a dietary tag from the input; this applies to halal-certified Japanese restaurants in Singapore and may not apply universally. Q8 fix (iteration 3): self-check performed on aliases_by_language vs flat aliases array — all strings present in aliases_by_language values confirmed to exist verbatim in the flat aliases array, including 'Teriyaki Chikin Teishoku' added to romanized key and flat aliases.
